codeReview.summary: The workflow correctly bootstraps launch, waits for runs to settle, and creates a provisional report, but its terminal-state decision is too optimistic. Once all replay work is terminal it always transitions to `analyzing` and then `completed`, even when the entire study only produced infrastructure failures.

issues.description: `completeStudyLifecycleAfterReplay` unconditionally transitions every fully-settled replaying study to `analyzing` and then `completed` after creating a report. There is no guard for the “all runs ended `infra_error`” path, so a study whose initial and replay runs all fail for infrastructure reasons still completes with a report instead of transitioning to `failed` as required by VAL-STUDY-065. The workflow therefore misclassifies platform outages as completed studies.

summary: Reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `86e3e54d1e2ad96717e59c4cc53e8e4fc58c6a23` for `study-lifecycle-workflow`. Result: fail — the replay-completion path always marks the study completed, even when every run ended in infrastructure failure.
